protocol.name: layer_surface_placement

description.summary: compositor-side placement of a layer surface within the usable area

protocol.description:
This protocol lets a layer shell surface ask the compositor to position it within the output's usable (non-exclusive) area — the output's logical geometry minus every exclusive zone reserved by other layer surfaces (panels, docks, bars) — instead of computing and applying margins itself.
A centered or fractionally-placed overlay (launcher, switcher, system dialog) otherwise has to learn the usable area, compute a margin, and apply it only after the surface is already mapped. The usable area is reported a frame or more after map (and again whenever the surface lands on a new output), so the surface visibly jumps from its first position to the corrected one. By describing the desired placement declaratively, the client lets the compositor position the surface at map time and keep it positioned across relayouts (panels appearing, resizing, or the surface moving to another output) with no client round-trip and no jump.
The placement only takes effect while the surface is not anchored on the axis it controls and reserves no exclusive zone; an anchored or exclusive surface is positioned by the standard layer-shell rules instead. Horizontal placement is left to the standard layer-shell rules — a surface that is not anchored horizontally is centered within the usable area.
This protocol is companion to layer_usable_area, which reports the usable area to clients that position themselves; here the compositor does the positioning. A client never needs both for the same surface.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: layer_surface_placement_manager_v1
interface.description: Manager for creating placement objects for layer shell surfaces.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer use this global. Existing placement objects remain valid until destroyed.

request.name: get_layer_surface_placement

description.summary: get a placement object for a surface

request.description:
Create a placement object for the given wl_surface, which must be a layer shell surface.
If the given wl_surface already has a layer_surface_placement_v1 object associated, the already_exists protocol error is raised.

entry.name: already_exists
entry.summary: surface already has a placement object

interface.name: layer_surface_placement_v1

description.summary: placement control for a layer surface

interface.description: Controls how the compositor positions the associated layer surface within its output's usable (non-exclusive) area.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Clears any compositor-side placement and reverts the surface to standard layer-shell positioning.

request.name: set_vertical_placement

description.summary: place the surface vertically within the usable area

request.description:
Position the surface's top edge at
usable_top + round(fraction * usable_height) + offset
clamped so the top edge is at least min_margin below the usable area's top edge, where usable_top/usable_height are that of the output the surface is shown on, in logical pixels.
The placement takes effect at the next layout pass and persists — it is re-applied on every relayout (a panel maps, unmaps, resizes, or the surface moves to another output) — until changed by another set_vertical_placement, cleared by unset_vertical_placement, or the object is destroyed.
This only takes effect while the surface is not anchored vertically (neither top nor bottom) and reserves no exclusive zone; otherwise the standard layer-shell positioning applies.
fraction is a wl_fixed value, normally in the range [0.0, 1.0]. offset and min_margin are signed/unsigned logical pixels respectively.

arg.name: fraction
arg.summary: fraction of usable-area height for the surface's top edge

arg.name: min_margin
arg.summary: minimum gap from the usable-area top, logical px

request.name: unset_vertical_placement
request.description: Revert the surface to standard layer-shell vertical positioning: anchor plus margin, or — for a surface not anchored vertically — vertical centering within the usable area.

request.name: set_max_height

description.summary: cap the surface height to a fraction of the usable area

request.description:
Clamp the surface's height to `max(round(fraction * usable_height), min_height)` logical pixels, where usable_height is that of the output the surface is shown on. This lets the client commit its full content height and have the compositor cap it to a fraction of the usable area (e.g. a grid that scrolls past 76% of the available height), without the client recomputing the cap from a usable-area value that is stale right after the surface appears on a new output.
Like set_vertical_placement, this is re-applied on every relayout and persists until changed by another set_max_height, cleared by unset_max_height, or the object is destroyed. It composes with set_vertical_placement and with vertical centering (the cap is applied before the surface is positioned).
fraction is a wl_fixed value, normally in the range [0.0, 1.0]. min_height is a floor (in logical px) for the resolved cap so a small output can't shrink the surface below a usable minimum; pass 0 for none.

arg.name: fraction
arg.summary: fraction of usable-area height to cap the surface to

arg.name: min_height
arg.summary: floor for the resolved cap, logical px

request.name: unset_max_height
request.description: Remove any height cap set by set_max_height; the surface is sized by the standard layer-shell rules (its requested size, clamped to the zone).

entry.name: surface_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated surface has been destroyed
